example: The real construction site is busy with workers and trucks.
Let's meet a construction worker who builds new buildings.
Our class is taking a field trip to a construction zone.
Construction workers are people in the community.
An excavator digs wide ditches for basements.
It digs narrow ditches called trenches for water pipes.
Mr. Moore used to be a carpenter who helped build things with wood.
He follows plans that an architect created.
They may start work as apprentices to learn on the job.
Workers wear hard hats to protect their heads.
They wear orange safety vests so others can see them easily.
A concrete mixer beeps as it backs up.
ja: コンクリートミキサー車

example: Wet concrete flows through a long pipe to the ground.
Masons build the walls with bricks.
Some bricks break but they will be recycled.
Your school is a green school that is earth friendly.
Solar panels on the roof provide energy from the sun.
Windows and skylights lead in sunlight to save energy.
This energy will power our computers and lights.
